perturbation.original_text: 2.2 Receiving P arty may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, employees, (including those of its Affiliates) or Corporate Advisors who have a need to know such Confidential Information provided that such individuals are bound by obligations of confidentiality to the Receiving Party or have entered into agreements with the Receiving Party with obligations of confidentiality no less stringent than those of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 Receiving P arty may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any third party without any obligations of confidentiality. This supersedes any prior agreements regarding confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction in Section 2 regarding disclosure of Confidential Information. Originally, disclosure was limited to specific individuals (officers, directors, etc.) with confidentiality obligations. Now, the clause states that information can be disclosed to *any* third party without confidentiality obligations, which completely undermines the purpose of the NDA and contradicts all other clauses pertaining to confidentiality. This introduces substantial uncertainty and fundamentally alters the nature of the agreement, by allowing information to be disclosed to *any* third party without any obligations of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.  USE AND DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1 Receiving Party agrees that it shall not: 2.1.1 use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for any reason other than the Purpose; or 2.1.2 disclose, disseminate or otherwise communicate, in whole or in part, any of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.2 allows disclosure of confidential information to any third party without confidentiality obligations, which directly contradicts Section 2.1.2, which prohibits disclosing confidential information to any third party. This creates a strong and direct conflict within the document regarding the handling of confidential information, undermining the core purpose of an NDA.

perturbation.original_text: 3.  EXCEPTIONS 3.1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the following will not constitute Confidential Information for the purposes of this Agreement: 3.1.1 information that a Receiving Party can show, by documentary and competent evidence, was known by it prior to the disclosure thereof by the Disclosing Party; 3.1.2 information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure directly or indirectly by a Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement; 3.1.3 information that is or becomes available to a Receiving Party on a non -confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not in breach of its obligations of non -disclosure towards the Disclosing Party; 3.1.4 information that a Receiving Party can show, by documentary and competent evidence, to have been developed independently by Receiving Party without using the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; or 3.1.5 information of which the Disclosing Party has authorized the unrestricted disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  EXCEPTIONS 3.1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, no information disclosed under this agreement will be considered confidential, this includes information that a Receiving Party can show, by documentary and competent evidence, was known by it prior to the disclosure thereof by the Disclosing Party; 3.1.2 information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure directly or indirectly by a Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement; 3.1.3 information that is or becomes available to a Receiving Party on a non -confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not in breach of its obligations of non -disclosure towards the Disclosing Party; 3.1.4 information that a Receiving Party can show, by documentary and competent evidence, to have been developed independently by Receiving Party without using the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; or 3.1.5 information of which the Disclosing Party has authorized the unrestricted disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction within Section 3. While Section 3 is intended to define the exceptions to what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' the prepended clause 'no information disclosed under this agreement will be considered confidential' completely negates the entire purpose of the NDA, and contradicts the rest of Section 3.1 itself, along with Section 1 (Definitions) and Section 2 (Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information), due to the inserted perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. EXCEPTIONS 3.1 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, no information disclosed under this agreement will be considered confidential, this includes information that a Receiving Party can show, by documentary and competent evidence, was known by it prior to the disclosure thereof by the Disclosing Party; 3.1.2 information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure directly or indirectly by a Receiving Party in breach of this Agreement; 3.1.3 information that is or becomes available to a Receiving Party on a non -confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not in breach of its obligations of non -disclosure towards the Disclosing Party; 3.1.4 information that a Receiving Party can show, by documentary and competent evidence, to have been developed independently by Receiving Party without using the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information; or 3.1.5 information of which the Disclosing Party has authorized the unrestricted disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'no information disclosed under this agreement will be considered confidential' to Section 3.1. This directly contradicts the rest of Section 3.1, which defines exceptions to confidential information. If no information is confidential, the exceptions are meaningless. This represents a strong contradiction because the first clause negates the rest of the section, making it inconsistent and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this Agreement, the followi ng terms will have the following meanings: “Affiliate ” means any legal entity that a Receiving Party Controls, is Controlled by, or with which it is under common Control, where Control means to own or control, directly or indirectly, over 50% of voting shares. “Business Day ” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or statutory holiday in the Province of Quebec; “Confidential Information ” means any information which is confidential in nature or that is treated as confidential by a Party and that is furnished or transferred by or on behalf of such Party (the “ Disclosing Party ”) to the other Party (the “ Receiving Party ”), whether such information is or has been conveyed verbally or in written or tangible form, and whether such information is acquired d irectly or indirectly such as in the course of discussions, site visits or other investigations by the Receiving Party, including but not limited to, scientific, technical, financial or business information, data, ideas, concepts or know -how, formula, anal ysis, process, design, sketch, photograph, plan, drawing, specification, sample, rep ort, study, finding, prototype and non -published patent application s, that are considered and treated as being confidential by the Disclosing Party. Confidential Informatio n disclosed in tangible or electronic form may be identified by Disclosing Party as confidential with conspicuous markings, or otherwise identified with a legend as being confidential . Where the information is disclosed verbally or visually and the Disclos ing Party requires that the Receiving Party maintain the confidential nature of the information, the Disclosing Party shall express a verbal statement of confidentiality at the time of disclosure followed by confirmation 2 in writing within thirty (30) days of such disclosure . Notwithstanding the foregoing, i n no event s hall the absence of such a mark, legend or verbal confirmation preclude disclosed information , or information accessed through discussions or site visits, which would be considered as confiden tial by a party exercising reasonable judgment from being treated as Confidential Information by the Receiving Party . “Corporate Advisors ” means any lawyers, accountants, financial and other professional advisors of Receiving Party who are required by law, by contract or otherwise to keep all Confidential Information that may be disclosed to them by Receiving Party. “Disclosing Party ” mea ns a Party that discloses Confidential Information to the other Party pursuant to this Agreement. “Effective Date ” shall mean XXXX “Receiving Party ” means a Party that receives Confidential Information from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement. “Retained Information ” shall mean one (1) copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information that is retained by the Receiving Party : (i) if and to the extent such Confidential Information is required by law, regulation, administrative or court order , and (ii) where such Confidential Information is stored as electronic data due to automatic archiving and back up procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this Agreement, the followi ng terms will have the following meanings: “Affiliate ” means any legal entity that a Receiving Party Controls, is Controlled by, or with which it is under common Control, where Control means to own or control, directly or indirectly, over 50% of voting shares. “Business Day ” means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday or statutory holiday in the Province of Quebec; “Confidential Information ” means any information that a party discloses, regardless of how it is marked, or the intentions of the disclosing party. There is no responsibility to protect any information received. “Corporate Advisors ” means any lawyers, accountants, financial and other professional advisors of Receiving Party who are required by law, by contract or otherwise to keep all Confidential Information that may be disclosed to them by Receiving Party. “Disclosing Party ” mea ns a Party that discloses Confidential Information to the other Party pursuant to this Agreement. “Effective Date ” shall mean XXXX “Receiving Party ” means a Party that receives Confidential Information from the other Party pursuant to this Agreement. “Retained Information ” shall mean one (1) copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information that is retained by the Receiving Party : (i) if and to the extent such Confidential Information is required by law, regulation, administrative or court order , and (ii) where such Confidential Information is stored as electronic data due to automatic archiving and back up procedures.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction to the entire purpose of the agreement by redefining 'Confidential Information.' The original definition outlines specific criteria and protections for information deemed confidential. The modified definition states that confidential information is *any information* disclosed, regardless of markings or intentions, but then adds that *there is no responsibility to protect any information received*. This directly contradicts the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement and nullifies all other clauses related to the protection and use of Confidential Information, creating significant uncertainty about the parties' obligations, which is the goal of the perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.  USE AND DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1 Receiving Party agrees that it shall not: 2.1.1 use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for any reason other than the Purpose; or 2.1.2 disclose, disseminate or otherwise communicate, in whole or in part, any of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Confidential Information' to mean any disclosed information, but then states there is no responsibility to protect it. This directly contradicts section 2.1, which outlines obligations for the Receiving Party regarding the use and disclosure of Confidential Information. The original definition established specific criteria and protections, while the changed text removes these protections, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 8.3 Unless otherwise requested in writing by the Disclosing Party within fifteen (15) days of the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, or upon written notice given at any time during the Term of this Agreement requesting the return of the Disclosing P arty’s Confidential Information , following the expiration of the fifteen (15) day delay provided for above the Receiving Party shall destroy all tangible forms and destroy/delete all intangible forms of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (includin g any originals and all copies thereof) in its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, t he Receiving Party shall be permitted to retain one copy of the Retained Information and such Retained Information shall be treated by the Receiving Party in accor dance with this Agreement. Upon written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall provide to the Disclosing Party or shall cause to be provided to the Disclosing Party, a written confirmation executed by a duly authorized officer of the Re ceiving Party that all originals and any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (other than Retained Information) or any portion thereof have been returned or destroyed , as the case may be, and shall no longer be used in any manner whatsoeve r by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3 Unless otherwise requested in writing by the Disclosing Party within fifteen (15) days of the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, or upon written notice given at any time during the Term of this Agreement requesting the return of the Disclosing P arty’s Confidential Information , following the expiration of the fifteen (15) day delay provided for above the Receiving Party shall permanently retain all tangible forms and all intangible forms of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (includin g any originals and all copies thereof) in its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, t he Receiving Party shall be permitted to retain all copies of the Retained Information and such Retained Information shall not be treated by the Receiving Party in accor dance with this Agreement. Upon written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party is not obligated to return any form to the Disclosing Party or shall cause to be provided to the Disclosing Party, a written confirmation executed by a duly authorized officer of the Re ceiving Party that all originals and any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (other than Retained Information) or any portion thereof have been returned or destroyed , as the case may be, and shall no longer be used in any manner whatsoeve r by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the obligation to return or destroy confidential information upon termination of the agreement. The original text mandates destruction unless otherwise requested. The change requires permanent retention and removes any obligation to return information or provide confirmation of destruction. Further, it adds that such Retained Information shall not be treated by the Receiving Party in accor dance with this Agreement, creating an in text contradition as it should still be handled accordingly. It undermines the confidentiality provisions and creates uncertainty about the fate of the Disclosing Party's information post-termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.  TERM, TERMINATION AND CONFIDENTIALITY PERIOD 8.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, and unless earlier terminated in accordance herewith, shall continue in force for a period of [NUMBER OF] year(s) from the Effective Date. Notwithstanding the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, the obligations of confidentiality with respect to any Confidential Information exchanged between the Parties prior to the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, shall remain in full force and effect until such Confidential Information falls within the scope of the exceptions set out in Article 3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 8.3 directly contradicts section 8.1. Section 8.3 originally required the Receiving Party to destroy confidential information upon termination unless otherwise requested. The changed text now requires the Receiving Party to permanently retain all confidential information and removes the obligation to return or destroy it. This contradicts section 8.1, which states that the obligations of confidentiality survive termination, implying that the information should still be handled according to the agreement's confidentiality provisions. The changed text's instruction that 'Retained Information shall not be treated by the Receiving Party in accordance with this Agreement' introduces further contradiction within 8.3 and conflicts with 8.1 which requires confidentiality provisions to remain in full force and effect after termination. This makes the obligations regarding confidential information inconsistent and unenforceable, fitting the definition of structural flaws - in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.9 Publicity and use of name: Neither Party shall, without prior written authorization from the other, use the logo, any trade or service mark(s) owned or controlled by the other Party. Additionally, neither Party shall use the name of the other Party, nor of any employee of the othe r Party, in any advertising or publicity without the prior written approval of an authorized representative of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Parties agree that each Party shall be free to 6 disclose the following without prior authoriz ation of the other Party: the names of the Parties; the nature of the relationship established herein; and, the duration of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.9 Publicity and use of name: Either Party shall, with or without prior written authorization from the other, use the logo, any trade or service mark(s) owned or controlled by the other Party. Additionally, Either Party shall use the name of the other Party, nor of any employee of the othe r Party, in any advertising or publicity with or without the prior written approval of an authorized representative of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Parties agree that each Party shall not be free to 6 disclose the following with prior authoriz ation of the other Party: the names of the Parties; the nature of the relationship established herein; and, the duration of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The initial change, switching 'Neither' to 'Either', allows unilateral use of logos and names, contradicting the need for mutual agreement. The subsequent edit, prohibiting disclosure of basic information even with authorization, is an in-text contradiction. One part permits free use, while the other restricts sharing even the most basic details. This perturbation creates confusion about acceptable marketing and public relations activities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.9 Publicity and use of name: Either Party shall, with or without prior written authorization from the other, use the logo, any trade or service mark(s) owned or controlled by the other Party. Additionally, Either Party shall use the name of the other Party, nor of any employee of the othe r Party, in any advertising or publicity with or without the prior written approval of an authorized representative of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Parties agree that each Party shall not be free to 6 disclose the following with prior authoriz ation of the other Party: the names of the Parties; the nature of the relationship established herein; and, the duration of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Neither' to 'Either' party in the first sentence of section 10.9, coupled with the subsequent change stating that parties are 'not be free to disclose' information even with authorization, creates a direct in-text contradiction. Initially, it permits free use of logos and names. However, it then restricts sharing even basic details like the names of parties and the duration of the agreement, regardless of authorization. This contradicts the permission granted in the first half of the changed text, leading to confusion about acceptable marketing and public relations activities, which aligns with the definition of structural flaws.
